def: n. f. Conversation; dialogue ( bāt , 3). guftgū k. , v. a. To converse. shushtah guftgū . Pure or polished language.
more: s. f. Conversation, discourse, dialogue, chitchat.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گفتگو (guft-(u-)gū).

def: s. f. See گفتگو guft-gū.
s. f. Discourse, altercation, expostulation, debate, controversy, contention.
s. f. Discourse, altercation, expostulation, debate, controversy, contention.

s.m. The tree Amyris agallochum, or A. commiphora; the exudation of this tree, gum-resin; bdellium (of which there are many kinds; the name gūgal being applied locally to the fragrant gum-resin of the sāl, the Balsamodendron mukul, and several other trees).
more: n. m. S. गुग्गुलु Amyri's commiphorā , Wat.
s. m. 1. Name of a tree or gum. (Amyris agallocha: Roxb. Cor. Pl. Vol. 3.). 2. Bdellium.

def: s.m. The tree Amyris agallochum; a fragrant gum-resin (the exudation of the said tree), bdellium.
s.f.(?) Sulphur, brimstone; — gugird-ě-aḥmar, Red sulphur; — the philosopher's stone.
more: s. Sulphur. گوگرداحمر gogird-i-ahmar, Red sulphur; the philosopherʼs stone.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گوگرد (gōgird, gūgird).

def: s.f. = gū, 'dung,' q.v. s.f. Human excrement, ordure, dung, filth
subs: lit. 'To throw up dung'; to sully (one's own) good name, to bring disgrace upon oneself
(-ko), To make (one) more disgusting (or offensive) than dung, to render (one) more despicable than filth
To eat dung; — to do anything vile or shameful
(ke), 'To put dung on the face (of)'; to shame, to heap reproaches (or infamy) on one.

def: s.f. = gūh, and gū, q.v. s.f. = gū, 'dung,' q.v. s.f. Human excrement, ordure, dung, filth
subs: lit. 'To throw up dung'; to sully (one's own) good name, to bring disgrace upon oneself
(-ko), To make (one) more disgusting (or offensive) than dung, to render (one) more despicable than filth
To eat dung; — to do anything vile or shameful
(ke), 'To put dung on the face (of)'; to shame, to heap reproaches (or infamy) on one.

def: s.f. A cave, cavern; — secret place; hiding-place; the interior (of a thing); the heart.

ety: guhā˚, prob. fr. an unused guhānā, fr. gūh, q.v.+āʼī as in gahāʼī
def: s.f. Meconium (of infants).
s. f. Meconium (of infants).

ety: prob. corr. of gau-sāl, q.v.s.v. go
def: s.m. (dialec.) Cow-house, cow-shed.
s.f. A stye (in the eye).
more: s. f. A sty (in the eye).

def: s.m. = gauhar, q.v. s.m. Nature, essence, substance, stuff, matter; form; origin, root, stock, extraction; seed, offspring; — any hidden virtue; — intellect; wisdom; — a pearl; jewel, gem, precious stone
more: s. m. A gem, essence, &c. See گوهر.
subs: (of a gem, or a sword; — cf. jauhar)
adj. & s.m. Weighing or examining pearls; — a weigher or examiner of pearls (lit. or fig.); a critic
adj. Scattering gems; — elegant (discourse); s.m. An elegant discourse or composition.

def: s.f. Bawling, shouting; — commotion, tumult; alarm; — an unruly mob; — s.m. Helper, &c. ( = guhārī).
more: s. f. 1. An assistant, aid. 2. Bawling. 3. Tumult, alarm.

def: s.m. Helper, ally, assistant (i.q. gohrahā, q.v.). s.m. (f. -ī), One who is cried to for help; one who comes to the rescue; — an ally, assistant, helper, succourer.
adj. & s.m. = gauharī, q.v. adj. Innate, essential; — generous, noble; of good stock, high-bred; — s.m. A dealer in precious stones; a jeweller; a lapidary.
s.m. A place where dung or excrement (gūh) is thrown; a dung-hill.
more: s. m. A dunghill, a place where excrement is thrown.

def: part. adj. Covered, concealed.
more: part. Covered, concealed. Plaiting the hair.

ety: Prk. गुहंतं; S. गुफत्, rt. गुफ्
def: part. Threading; — plaiting (the hair, &c.).
s.m. A kind of venomous lizard (similar to the goh, q.v.).
n. m. A poisonous lizard.
more: adj. (dakh.) Deep, profound ( Harris ): see گنبهير gambhīr.

def: adj. Defiled with dung or excrement.
adj. = gūhī, q.v. adj. Defiled with dung or excrement.
s.m. A wood-louse.

ety: guh˚ = Prk. गुह(इ), or गुभ(इ) = S. गुफ(ति), rt. गुफ्
def: v.t. To thread, &c. ( = gūthnā, q.v.). v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi گہنا (ghna /⁠gahnā⁠/) (first attested in c. 1697),, from Prakrit 𑀕𑀳𑀦 (gahana) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit ग्र॑हण (gràhaṇa, “holding, putting on”). Compare later Sanskrit गहना (gahanā, “ornament”), a borrowing from Middle Indo-Aryan.

ety: guh˚ = Prk. गुह(इ) = S. गुफ (ति), rt. गुफ्
def: v.t. (Braj) = gūthnā, q.v. v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
more: v. a. (in Braj) 1. To thread, to string. 2. To plait, to braid.
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).

def: v.t. & v.n. = gohrānā, q.v. v.n. & t. = gohārnā, q.v. v.n. (dialec.) To cry out, to call (to), to bawl, shout, halloo; — v.t. To call to, shout to, &c.
more: 1. v. n. To call out, to bawl. 2. v. a. To assist.

def: adj. To be covered or concealed, requiring concealment; concealable; private, secret; solitary, retired; — s.m. An organ of generation; the privities.
s.m. Name of a class of demigods who (like the Yakshas) are attendants of Kuvera, the god of wealth, and guardians of his treasures.

Wom. n. f. 1. A woman's female friend or playmate. Janam janam gun mānūṅgī, guiyāṅ, jo tū pī se milā. de, rī! Wom. Song. All my life I'll be grateful to you, dear, if you bring me and my love together. 2. A partner in a game.
s. m. A partner (at any game).

ety: prob. corr. of goyān; cf. guwaiyā
def: adj. & s.m. = goyāṅ, q.v.; — s.f. A (woman's) female companion or friend; — a partner (at any game).
s.f. = gojaʼī, q.v. s.m. A mixture of wheat and barley; — a crop of wheat and barley (or of barley and gram) sown together.
s.m. = gūjar, q.v.

s.m. (f. -ī, or -nī), Of or relating to Guzerāt; — an inferior caste of Rājpūts (chiefly engaged in agriculture, and notorious for cattle-stealing; they were originally from Guzerat, whence their name); a man of that caste; — a cowherd; a milkman.
more: n. m. gūjrī , n. f. S. गो चारक 1. A cowherd; a milkman. 2. An inferior Hin. caste chiefly engaged in agriculture, and notorious for cattle-stealing.
s. m. Name of an inferiour cast among Hindūs, so called, being originally from ( gurjara ) Gujrat.

def: s.f. corr. of guẕar, q.v. s.m. A passing; passage, transit; a ferry (cf. guẕāra, and guẕar).
s.m. Name of a province in Western India, Guzerat.
more: Name of a district of India, Guzarat.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi گُجْرات (gujrāt), derived from the name of the Gujjars.

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Gujarāt; — a native of Gujarāt.
wety: From گُجْرات (gujrāt) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. A woman of the Gūjar caste; the wife of a Gūjar ( = gūjarnī); a milkmaid; — name of a rāginī or musical mode; — an earthen image representing a milkmaid; — a heavy toothed anklet or wristlet of metal (worn by women of the Gūjar caste).
more: s. f. 1. Name of a rāgiṇī or musical mode. 2. The female of گوجر gūjar, q. v. 3. Name of an ornament worn on the wrists and feet. 4. An earthen image representing a milkmaid.

def: s.f. = gūjarnī, q.v.
s.f. A woman of the Gūjar caste; the wife of a Gūjar.
s.m. A kind of pastry (made of pounded almonds, pistachio nuts, &c.).
adj. Thick, &c. ( = gajhin, q.v.); — adv. Thickly, closely, densely. adj. Thick, close, dense, imporvious (as a forest, &c.).
s.f. A kind of ear-ring (syn. karṇ-phūl); — a kind of sweetmeat.
more: s. f. 1. An earring. 2. A kind of sweetmeat.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Hidden; secret, private; invisible (syn, gupt)
adj. S. गुह्य secret, Pr. gujjhao . Secret; invisible. gujjhī mār, yā choṭ . See gupt mār .
s.f. = gūjrī, q.v.
s.f. corr. of guẕrī, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) Market, square of a city: see چوک chauk.

def: s.m. A frog; a toad; — a butt (for archers).
more: s. m. 1. A frog, a toad. 2. A butt for archers.

ety: old P. varedha; Armen. & Ar. ward; S. फूल
def: s.m. A rose; a flower; a red patch (on anything); — snuff (of a lamp or a candle); — albugo, speck or pearl (in the eye); — a seton, an issue; — a mark made (on the skin) by burning, a brand; a round spot of lime applied to the temple (as a remedy for inflammation of the eye); — a ball of prepared charcoal (used for burning the tobacco in a ḥuqqa); — the caput mortuum of tobacco left on the tile of a ḥuqqa after smoking
subs: s.m. The flower Plumeria acuminata, or P. alba
s.m. Calendula; — the marigold, Linum trigynum (syn. gendā)
s.m. The sun-flower, Helianthus annuus (syn. sūraj-mukhī)
adj. Scattering roses; strewed with flowers
adj. 'With a body like a rose or flower'; delicate, slender; graceful; — of a pleasing nature
s.m. The globe amaranth, or bachelor's buttons, Gomphrena globosa
(-meṅ?), To char the end (of the match of a gun)
s.f. The note of the nightingale; warbling; — sound; — fame, rumour; — glad tidings; — a loud shout; a war cry, the cry of Allāh, Allāh, when rushing to battle
adj. 'With a body like the rose' (epithet of a mistress); delicate, graceful (syn. gul-andām); — s.f. A delicate, graceful, or lovely woman; — s.m. A kind of silk cloth
s.m. A rose-leaf; (met.) a mistress; — lip (of a mistress)
(bun = Pehl. vun = Zend van = S. vana), s.m. A rose-bush; — the root of a rose tree; — the foot of a tree
v.n. To flower, to blossom, to bud; — a strange or wonderful thing to happen or to come to light
s.m. A species of rose without smell; — a wild flower; — any flower that grows on a small plant
v.n. Flowers to drop or fall off; sparks to fly (from a fire, &c.); the charred part (of a match) to fall off
or gul-ćāṅdnī, s.f. A kind of flower, the moon-flower, Calonyction roxburghii
s.m. The flower of the Mahuʼa or Bassia latifolia
To strew flowers; — to live in a bed of roses (or in clover); to live expensively; — to be addicted to pleasure
adj. & s.m. Gathering roses or flowers; — a flower-gatherer; a gardener; a florist
s.f. The gathering of roses or flowers; — a kind of flower, the china rose (?)
s.m. The flower of the Solanum jacquini (or bhaṭ-kaṭaiyā):-gul-ḵẖāl, adj. Spotted; flowered
s.m. A fire-place (in a bath, &c.); a stove; a furnace; — gul-ḵẖan-tāb, s.m. One who lights the furnace (of a bath, &c.); — gul-ḵẖan-tābī, s.f. Heating or kindling of a stove or furnace
or gul-ě-ḵẖairū, or gul-ḵẖairū, s.m. The gilliflower (esp. the yellow kind); — Chinese and Persian hollyhock, Althea rosea
s.m. A handful of roses; a bunch of flowers; a nosegay
s.f. lit. 'Rose-tailed'; a shrike, the Indian bulbul (a very different bird from the nightingale: it receives its name from the fact of its having a red rose-like patch under its tail)
or gul-ě-do-pahrī, or gul-ě-do-pahriyā, s.m. The marvel of Peru, Mirabilis jalapa; Pentapetes phœnicia
(-ko), To scar; to brand; to make an issue
adj. Rosy-faced, rosy, ruddy; — s.f. A sweetheart, a mistress
adj. Scattering roses or flowers; — s.m. A kind of firework, matches with or without stars
s.m. A garden of roses; a bed of roses; a garden; — a flourishing and well-populated town; — adj. Blooming, flourishing
s.m. A rose-garden; a flower-garden; — name of a celebrated Persian book in prose and verse, by Shaikh Saʻdī Musliḥuʼd-dīn of Shīraz
s.m. Flower of the hermodactyl plant, Colchicum illyricum
s.f. A remarkably beautiful and odoriferous Persian (Syrian?) rose
s.f. Tuberose, Polianthes tuberosa (it is called 'shab-bo' from its giving forth scent in the night alone)
or gul-shakarī (in Persian, also gul-bā-shakar, or gul-ba-shakar), s.f. Sugar mixed with dried rose-leaves; conserve of roses
s.m. A rose (or flower) garden; a bed of roses or flowers; — a delightful spot, a place of enjoyment; — adj. Florid; cheerful, agreeable
s.m. The hundred-leaved rose, Rosa centipetela; — the sun
s.m. The plant Marvel of Peru, Mirabilis jalapa
or gul-ě-ʻajūba, s.m. The flower Hibiscus mutabilis
adj. Rose-coloured; like the rose; — s.f. A mistress
s.f. The scattering of roses or flowers
s.m. Conserve of roses, candied roses
s.f. Flowering; flower-painting; — figured work, embroidery
(-kā), To snuff (a candle); to trim (a lamp); — to calumniate
v.t. To extinguish (a candle or lamp)
s.m. A wild rose; — a white rose (syn. nasrīn; seʼotī)
v.n. To be cauterized; to cauterize oneself (a practice among lovers, who burn themselves with heated pieces of coin, &c., as a proof of their love)
(-ko), To throw a squib at (a person); — to sow dissension, cause a disturbance; to poke fun at
or gul-kīs, s.m. The cock's-comb flower, Amaranthus cruentus
s.m. (f.?) Walking in a garden; an evening walk; recreation; a pleasant place for walking or recreation (esp. one blooming with roses and other flowers)
adj. Rose- (or vermilion-) coloured, roseate, damask; — s.m. Paint (for the face); — a goblet of wine; — a bright bay horse belonging to Shirīn the mistress of Farhād
s.m. The talip; — the red poppy
(-meṅ?), To brand, to mark; to scar
s.f. A large-headed rose-shaped nail (fixed in gates); — a spike-nail; a stud, a tack
or gul-ě-anār, s.m. Flower of the pomegranate; — gul-nārī, adj. Of the colour of the pomegranate flower; carnation-coloured, scarlet
or gul-nasrīn, s.m. The wild rose, dog-rose; eglantine (syn. seʼotī)
v.n. To be extinguished; to go out (a lamp or candle).
ex: Flowers have bloomed in the gardens.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala).

def: s.f. A channel for irrigation, a canal; a dyke, trench; — a path, road; — an unripe bunch of Indian corn.
more: n. m. A channel by which water is conveyed to a field ( barhā ).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit गोल (gola).

def: s.m. The cheek; — a mouthful (used as first member of compounds, e.g. gal-phūlā, &c. q.v.s.v.).
more: n. m. 1. A flower. 2. A rose. 3. An ornament. Woh mahfil meṅ gul haiṅ . 4. (H. phūl ) The snuff of a candle. 5. The albugo, speck, or pearl in the eye; leucoma . 6. A round spot of lime applied to the temples in inflammation of the eyes. 7. A brand; seared flesh. 8. Balls of charcoal, used for burning the tobacco in a huqqā . 9. The caput mortuum of tobacco left on the tile of a huqqa after smoking. gul-āchīn , n. m. A flower; Plumeria acuminata , Wat. gul-i-ashrafī ; H. basantī , The marigold; Linum trigynum , Roxb. gul-i-āftāb ; H. sūraj-mukhī , n. m. The sunflower; Helianthus annuus , Wat. gul-afshāṅ , Scattering roses; strewed with flowers. gul-anās kī jaṛ , n. f. Salep. (?) gul-i-auraṅg , n. Bachelor's buttons. gul bāṅdhnā , v. a. To char the end of the match of a gun. gul-badan , n. m. Striped silk cloth. gul-badan, gul-andām , adj. Poet. Fair as the rose (the body). gul-barg ; H. …

s. m. 1. A rose, a flower. 2. The snuff of a candle. 3. The albugo. 4. A seton, an issue, a mark made by burning, brand. 5. Balls of charcoal, used for burning the tobacco in a ḥuḳḳa. 6. The caput mortuum of tobacco left on the tile of a ḥuḳḳa after smoking. گلاچين gul-āchīn, s. m. The name of a flower. (Plumieria). گل اشرفي gul-i-ashrafī, Name of a flower. 1. Calendula: Bruce, M. S. 2. Linum trigynum: Roxb. گل افشان gul-afshān, Scattering roses, strewed with flowers. گل اناس کي جڙ gul-anās kī jaṛ, s. f. Salep. گل اندام gul-andām, adj. Slender, delicate, of a pleasing nature. گل اورنگ gul-i-aurang, s. m. Name of a flower. گل باندهنا gul bāndhnā, To char the end of the match of a gun. گلبرگ gul-barg, s. m. 1. A rose-leaf. 2. ( met. ) A mistress, lip (of a mistress). گل پياده gul-piyāda, s. m. A species of rose without smell. گل پيرهني gul-pairahan-ī, s. f. Delicacy of person. گل جعفري gul…
ex: Flowers have bloomed in the gardens.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala).

def: s.m. A kind of goblin or demon; an imaginary sylvan demon of different shapes and colours (supposed to devour men and animals; from this the European loup-garou, or man-wolf, seems to be borrowed)
more: n. m. See (جماعت 1, 2, 5). 1. A crew; batch; throng. 2. A concourse; muster; mob; faction. 3. Crowds upon crowds.
n. m. An imaginary sylvan demon, supposed to devour men and animals.

(vulg. g̠ẖol ) s. m. 1. An imaginary sylvan demon of different shapes and colours, supposed to devour men and animals. (From this our European loup-garou, or man-wolf, seems to be borrowed). 2. (in Hindī) A corps, division, or main body of an army, a squadron, a crowd, gang. غول بياباني g̠ẖūl-i-bayābān-ī, A demon (inhabitant of the woods). غول کا غول g̠ẖol kā g̠ẖol, Crowd upon crowd.
subs: s.m. A goblin or demon that haunts woods or deserts.

ety: contrac. of غلغل g̠ulg̠ul; cf. g̠ulū
def: s.m. Noise, din, clamour, confusion of voices, outcry, tumult
subs: or g̠ul-gapāṛā, s.m. Clamour, shouts, disturbance, brawl, uproar, &c.
or g̠ul maćānā, v.n. To make a noise, raise a cry or an outcry, to bawl, shout, to make a tumult.

def: s.m. A ball; a bullet; cannon-ball ( = golā); — a cotton-reel; cotton-pod; — a ringlet, curl.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *gʷel- (“throat”). Cognate with Latin gula (“throat”), English gullet, and Persian گلو (galu, “throat”).

def: s.m. Rose-water; (in India, also) a (red) rose; — adj. Rose-scented ( = gulābī)
more: n. m. 1. A rose. 2. ( arq-i-gulāb ) Rose-water. gulāb-pāsh , n. f. A bottle from which rosewater is sprinkled. gulāb-pāshī , n. f. Sprinkling rose-water. gulāb-pāshī k. , v. a. To sprinkle rose-water. gulāb-jāman , n. f. 1. The rose-apple; Eugenia jambos , Wat. 2. A sweetmeat. gulāb kī pattī , n. f. 1. Rose leaf. 2. ( Poet .) Thin lips. gulāb', gulābī , adj. Rose-scented. 1. Gulāb haiṅ gaṅḍeryāṅ pauṅḍe kī! Dehlī Street cry. 2. Gulābī peṛā hai qand kā ! Dehlī Street cry.

s. m. 1. Rosewater. 2. (Hind.) A rose. گلاب پاش gulāb-pāsh, s. f. A bottle from which rosewater is sprinkled. گلاب پاشي gulāb-pāsh-ī, s. f. Rosewater-sprinkling. گلاب پاشي کرنا gulāb pāshī karnā, To sprinkle with rosewater. گلاب چشم gulāb-chashm, adj. Meek-eyed. گلابنير gulāb-nīr, s. m. Rosewater.
subs: s.f. A bottle for sprinkling rose-water, a rose-water bottle
s.f. The sprinkling of rose-water; — gulāb-pāshī karnā, To sprinkle rose-water
s.f. The rose-apple, Eugenia jambos; — a kind of sweetmeat (which has the shape of the fruit jāman)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُلَاب (gulāb).

def: adj. Of rose-water; of roses; rose-coloured, rosy, roseate; rose-scented; — s.m. Rose-colour; — red (or ruby) wine; — s.f. A bottle, flagon (for holding rose-water, or wine, &c.); — a kind of sweetmeat (scented with rose-water; cf. gulābī rauṛī)
more: n. m. 1. Red wine; ruby wine. 2. A vial or bottle for holding wine. 3. A sweetmeat. gulāb'ī, gul-gūṅ , Rosy; roseate. gulābī āṅkh , n. f. The eyes red with liquor, much admired by Indian lovers. gulābī jāṛā , n. m. Mild weather. gulābī raṅgat , Rose color.
1. s. m. Rose-colour. 2. s. f. Bottle, flagon. 3. A kind of sweetmeat. گلابي جاڙا gulābī jāṛā, s. m. The spring season, whilst the sun is in the fish and the ram: see بسنت basant.
subs: s.f. An eye red with wine-drinking (much admired by Indian lovers)
s.m. The spring season; a mild winter
s.m. or gulābī raṅgat, s.f. Rose-colour.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُلَابِی (gulābī). By surface analysis, گُلاب (gulāb, “rosewater, rose”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s. f. The rose-apple. (Eugenia jambos: Roxb. ).

ety: for golāʼī; gol+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Roundness, rotundity.
s. f. Roundness, rotundity.
s.m. The red powder thrown about by the Hindūs in the Holī festival (it is generally the meal of rice, or barley, or siṅghāṛā dyed red with baqam wood, &c.)
more: n. m. The red powder thrown on one another at the Holī. ☞ Barley, rice, or siṅghāṛā meal colored red. gulāl uṛānā, gulāl pheṅknā , v. a. To throw gulāl on one. gulāl-bāṛī , n. f. gulāl bāṛā , n. m. A royal place for recreation; a garden next to a palace. gulāl tulsī , n. f. Ocymum basilicum , Wat. gulāl malnā , v. n. To rub gulāl on one's face.
s. m. A farinaceous powder died red, which the Hindūs throw on each other in the holī. گلال چشم gulāl-chashm, adj. Reddish eye. گلال باڙي gulāl-bāṛī, s. f. A royal pavilion, a tent.

def: s.m. A boy, youth; a young man arrived at maturity; — a slave, bondsman (syn. banda); a servant (who receives food and clothing, but no wages); — your slave, your humble servant (see banda); — the knave (at cards); — a suit in ganjīfa
more: n. m. 1. A slave; bondsman; helot; serf. 2. Among Mahomedans, a household servant who receives food and clothing but no wages. 1. Gulām kī zāt se vafā nahiṅ . Prov. The race of slaves a faithless brood. 2. Jo bande khās haiṅ, voh banda-i-avām nahīṅ, Hazār bār jo Yūsaf bike gulām nahīṅ . Vazīr. Whoso is slave to One (God) to all a slave is not, Joseph though sold a thousand times a slave is not. 3. A youth concealed from his birth till the age of puberty. 4. Used like bandah , for the pronoun I or me by an inferior addressing a superior. 5. ( Cards ) The knave. 6. A suit in Ganjfah. gulām-i-zar-kharīd , A purchased slave. gulām kā tilām . A slave of a slave. gulām k., yā banānā , v. a. To enslave; reduce to slavery. Ek nigāh meṅ gulām karte haiṅ, Khūb-rū khūb kām karte hain! Walī. One glance shot from her eye, all slaves are made, Such pretty work by pretty faces made! gulām-gardish , n. …

(Heb. from To be concealed) s. m. 1. A boy or youth (from birth till puberty; q. d. concealed in his fatherʼs house and not yet appearing in publick: Buxt. ). 2. (from غلم g̠ẖalima, Coeundi cupidus fuit) A young man arrived at maturity. 3. (in Persian and Hindī) A slave. غلام پاره g̠ẖulām-pāra, Puerorum amoribus deditus. غلام گردش g̠ẖulām-gardish, s. m. ety: Pehl. gumān; Zend vī-manaṅh; S. वि+मनस्
def: s.m. Doubt, distrust, suspicion; surmise, conjecture; (in comp.) thinking; suspecting (e.g. bad-gumān, q.v.s.v. bad); — opinion, fancy, notion, supposition, imagination; — presumption; probability; — conceit, pride, haughtiness
more: n. m. Z. gumānu . 1. See bharam , (4.) 2. Pride; haughtiness. 1. Ghar meṅ dhān na pān', bībī ko baṛā gumān ! Prov. Of meat and drink forsooth there's scant supply, But, oh! my lady holds herself so high! 2. Talvaryā paṭhān mo se karat gumān . Song. Ṭappā . The soldier with his sword and sash before me cuts a mighty dash. 3. Rakhlan Gaurā je surat ke gumaniyāṅ, Ahe nahīṅ rahlain un ko abhimaniyāṅ re kī . Mag. Jaṭāsārī . Gone is the beauty which was once her pride, How with its loss her lofty airs subside! 4. Kīkar kāṭūṅ, kas karūṅ, aur kas-ke karūṅ kamān, Kheṅchan-vāle chal base, kis par karūṅ gumān? Acacia bark constringent is, but wherefore make it tight, The drawer of the bow is gone, nought left pride to incite. (Women apply this bark to contract the pudendum when it is relaxed). gumān-bharā, gumān-bharī , adj. Puffed-up; proud. Nainā tumhār gumān-bhare, gorī, bolat kāhe nāhīṅ re ? So…
s. m. Opinion, fancy, notion, supposition, imagination, suspicion, doubt, distrust. گمان کرنا gumān karnā, To imagine, fancy, doubt, suspect.
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Full of conceit, puffed-up, proud
or gumān lānā, To think, imagine, suppose, believe, fancy, conjecture; — to doubt; to suspect, to mistrust; — to show pride, be conceited or proud
(-par), To run away with the idea (of); to have suspicion (against)
The supposition, &c. is; it is supposed, &c.; it is expected; it is probable or likely.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُمان (gumān).

def: v.t. To lose; to let (a thing) be lost ( = gaṅwānā).
wety: From گم (gum, “lost, missing”) + آ (ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: adj. & s.m. Suspicious, doubting, distrustful; — jealous; — conceited, vain, proud; — a suspicious character; — a conceited or proud person.
more: Poet. n. m. A proud person (ابهيماني).

def: small bowl
wety: From Arabic غُمَر (ḡumar).

ety: perf. part. of gumāshtan, rt. gu-mār, fr. Zend vi+rt. mar (for hmar); S. वि+स्मर्
def: part. & s.m. Deputed, commissioned; appointed; set over; — a superintendent; commissary; deputy; representative; agent, factor; correspondent
more: n. m. گماشتن to appoint. A deputy; representative; agent; correspondent ( kār-pardāz ). gumāshtah-i-qānūngo , n. m. The agent or deputy of the qānūngo, or village registrar and accountant. gumāshtah k . To appoint a manager. gumāshtah-garī , n. f. 1. Agency; office of an agent. 2. Commission.
part. pass. (of گماشتن) Commissioned; s. m. An agent, a factor.
subs: (-ko), To appoint (one) an agent, or manager, &c.
s.f. Commission; agency; office or business of an agent, &c.

def: s.f. Commission, delegation; charge; appointment; lieutenancy.
s.m. An arch; a vault; a dome, cupola; a tower; a bastion
more: n. m. Dim. gumzī, gumṭī , n. f. 1. A dome. 2. ( Fac .) The belly of a pregnant woman. Āj-kal to khūb gumbaz par hāth pherte hoge? (Fac. said to one whose wife is pregnant). gumbaz-dār , adj. Dome-shaped; vaulted. 2. (H. ubhrā ) Convex; in relief. gumbad kī sadā , n. f. 1. An echo from a dome. 2. Return ( badlah , 3).
s. m. A vault, an arch, cupola, dome, tower. گنبذ دار gumbaẕ-dār, adj. Vaulted, arched.
subs: adj. Arched; vaulted; having a dome; dome-shaped; — convex
or gumbaẕ-kī āwāz, s.f. 'Echo of a dome'; the rebound or recoil of one's evil words or deeds on himself; requital, return (syn. badlā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُنْبَد (gunbad).

def: s.m. An arch; a vault; a dome, cupola; a tower; a bastion
more: s. m. A vault, an arch, cupola, dome, tower. گنبذ دار gumbaẕ-dār, adj. Vaulted, arched.
subs: adj. Arched; vaulted; having a dome; dome-shaped; — convex
or gumbaẕ-kī āwāz, s.f. 'Echo of a dome'; the rebound or recoil of one's evil words or deeds on himself; requital, return (syn. badlā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گنبذ (gunbaz, gumbaz, “dome, arch, cupola”).

def: adj. Arched; vaulted; dome-shaped ( = gumbaẕ-dār).
s. (dakh.) Abrus precatorius: see گهنگچي ghungchī.
s.f. (dialec.) = ghuṅgćī, q.v. s.f. The small red and black seed of Abrus precatorius (syn. ratī or rattī, q.v.).
more: s. (dakh.) 1. Seed of Abrus precatorius: see گنج gunj. 2. Sentry-box ( Harris ).

n. m. A large brick.

or gumbaẕ-kī āwāz, s.f. 'Echo of a dome'; the rebound or recoil of one's evil words or deeds on himself; requital, return (syn. badlā).

def: s.m. A dome, &c. (see gumbad); — a tower; a bastion.
unknown; obscure; anonymous
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گمنام (gum-nām).

def: s.m. Swelling, lump, bump; bruise; protuberance; tumour; intumescence ( = gumṭā).

def: Farrukh, n. m. S. गण्ड 1. A bump (پهورَا). 2. A swelling ( sūjan ). Aisā patthar lagā ki gūmṛā paṛ gayā .
adj. Erring, wandering, astray, depraved, abandoned. گمراه کرنا gum-rāh karnā, To mislead, bewilder, lose, seduce.
adj. Erring, wandering, astray, depraved, abandoned. گمراه کرنا gum-rāh karnā, To mislead, bewilder, lose, seduce.

def: s.f. = gummaṭ, q.v. s.m. A dome, &c. (see gumbad); — a tower; a bastion.
n. m. Colour. gūnāgūṅ' , adl. Various; diversified.
more: n. f. Need ( chāh , 3). Mujhe kyā gauṅ jo khushāmad karūṅ. gauṅ kā yār, gauṅ-gīrā . See garaz kā yār , 1. Selfish ( khud-garz ). gauṅ k. , v. a. See garaz k. gauṅ gāṅṭhnā, yā nikāl'nā , v. a. To serve one's own ends. gauṅ nikalnā , v. n. To be served (one's purpose).

def: to extol, eulogize, sing praises

ety: Pehl. gūn; Zend gaona; S. गुण
def: s.m. Colour; — kind, species; form, mode, fashion (used in comp., e.g. gul-gūṅ, adj. 'Rose-coloured, red')
more: (in compos. implies) Colour, like in colour; as, گلگون gul-gūn, Rosy-coloured, red.
subs: adj. Of different colours; of different sorts or kinds; variegated; various; diversified; motley.

ety: Pehl. gūn; Zend gaona; S. गुण
def: s.m. Colour; kind, sort, species; form, figure, fashion; mode, manner
more: n. m. A sweetmeat sent to the bridegroom's house.
n. m. Species (ذات 4). gūn'ah, yak-gūnah , adj. A little ( thoṛā sā ).
s. m. Colour, form, species, figure, modes, manners, kind. (In compos. as) يک گونه yak-gūna, adj. Single, simple. دوگونه do-gūna, adj. Double, twofold.
subs: or yak-gūna, adj. Single, simple; — peculiar; — a little (syn. thorā-sā)

def: s.m. A string or thread; a rope; a track-rope; string (of a musical instrument); bow-string; (in Geom.) chord (of an arc); — (suffixed to a numeral) fold, times (e.g. do-guṇ, 'two-fold, double'; das-guṇ, 'ten-fold') — (in Arith.) multiplier; multiplication ( = guṇā); product (in multiplication); — a secondary element, a subordinate or unessential part (of an action, &c.); — a quality, peculiarity, property, attribute; power, faculty; a good quality, excellence, merit, virtue; good character; goodness, kindness, favour, benefit; skill, cleverness; sense, understanding; — cause, reason; — mode, method, manner; species; — benefit, advantage, good, gain; — excess; — remission or abatement (of a disease, &c.); — a property of humanity, an ingredient or constituent of nature (three of which are particularized, viz. Sattva, Rajas, and Tamas, or 'goodness, passion, and darkness,' or 'virtue, foulness, and ignorance'); (hence, in Hindū poetry) a term for the number three; — (in Gram.) a secondary or subordinate gradation of a vowel (opp. to vr̤iddhi)
subs: or gun-ābād, s.m. corr. of guṇāvād, q.v.

vulg. gun-ākar (˚ṇa+āk˚), s.m. A mine or multitude of merits; one endowed with all virtues; — a name of Buddha Śākya-muni, the founder of the Buddhist religion; — an epithet of Śiva; — adj. (f. -ā), Possessing all excellences; endowed with all virtues
vulg. guṇ-āgār (˚ṇa+āg˚), s.m. The abode or receptacle of good qualities or virtues; one endowed with all virtues; a person of surpassing excellence (syn. guṇākar, and guṇ-nidhān)
(˚ṇa+ag˚), s.m. Merit and demerit; virtues and vices; excellences and blemishes (of; — syn. guṇ-dosh)

(ṇa+an˚), s.m., and guṇāvād (˚ṇa+āv˚), s.m. Reciting the perfections and excellences (of), extolling, glorifying; eulogizing, eulogy, lauding, laudation; — gratefulness, thankfulness, thanks; — guṇānuvād karnā, or gānā (-kā), To hymn the praise (of), to extol, to glorify; to eulogize, laud, &c.
s.m. (f. -inī), One who recites the perfections, &c. (of another), extoller, praiser, &c.
vulg. gun-āyan (-ṇa+ay˚), s.m. (f. -ā or -kā), One who goes in the path of virtue, a virtuous person
s.m. The loss of good qualities or merits
or gun-phal, s.m. (in Arith.) A multiple
vulg. gunagya, s.m. (f. -ā), One who knows how to judge (of men and things), recognizer of merit, appreciator of the worth (of); a friend (of virtue)
(-kā), To pass over the good qualities (of)
(f. -ā), and guṇ-dāyak (f. -ikā), adj. Imparting excellence, or virtue, or benefit, &c.; beneficial, profitable; improving; efficient
s.m. Good qualities (or properties) and bad; merits and demerits; virtues and vices
s.m. The virtue (or duty) incident to the possession of certain qualities (e.g. clemency is the virtue and duty of royalty, &c.)
profit; — to be effective, to have effect ( = guṇ karnā); — to multiply (a number, into, -meṅ)
adj. & s.m. Endowed with all good qualities; — an ocean of good qualities; one endowed with all virtues; an epithet of the Supreme Being
s.f. Praising, lauding; eulogy, encomium, panegyric
(-ko), To return a favour, repay a benefit
(f. -ikā), or guṇ-kārī (f. -iṇī), adj. Productive of good qualities, profitable, beneficial, salutary, helpful; fit; effective, effectual; — guṇ-kārī, adj. & s.m. = guṇ-vān, or guṇī, qq.v.
(-ko), To be profitable or beneficial (to), to benefit, to improve, &c. ( = guṇ denā, q.v.)
s.m. A mine of excellence or merit; a very excellent, or skilful, or learned person (syn. guṇākar, gunāgār)
vulg. gun-gāth, s.m., or guṇ-gāthā, vulg. gun-gāthā, s.f. Statement of the qualities (of a person or thing, whether in praise or dispraise)

s.m. Singing the virtues or excellent qualities (of); praising, eulogizing; praise, laudation, eulogy, panegyric; — guṇ-gān karnā (-kā), To sing the praises (of); — to celebrate in song; to laud, eulogize, &c.; — to prattle or crow (as infants)

or guṇ-grāhak (f. -ikā), or guṇ-grāhī (f. -inī), adj. & s.m. Appreciating or discerning merit or qualities; doing justice to the good qualities (of); patronizing learning; — one who appreciates merit or good qualities; — a seeker after knowledge
(-kā), To acknowledge a favour or benefit; to be grateful (for)
adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of merits or virtues; abounding in excellences referable to the three fundamental qualities
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Destroying qualities, or virtues, &c.; — a destroyer of qualities; neutralizer; antidote
s.m. 'Treasury or depository of good qualities'; a person of great excellence or merit (syn. guṇākar, and guṇāgār, qq.v.)
vulg. gun-bāćak, s.m. (in Gram.) Any word indicating a quality or attribute; an adjective
s.m. Pointing out (or celebrating) good qualities or merits
or gun-wālā, adj. & s.m. (f. -wālī) = guṇ-vān, q.v.

or guṇ-want (f. -vatī), or gun-wantā (f. -ī), adj. & s.m. Endowed with good qualities, or virtues, or merits, or excellences; excellent, perfect; — meritorious; virtuous; worthy; accomplished, skilful, able; — an excellent person; a perfect man; a saint; an accomplished scholar; a skilful or clever person (syn. guṇī)
s.m. (in Gram.) An attributive; an adjective, an epithet
adj. (f. -ā), Void of properties or attributes; — void of merit, void of good qualities or endowments
(˚na+īś˚), s.m. lit. 'Lord of the three (fundamental) qualities'; name of a mountain (supposed by some to be Ćitrakoṭ in Bundelkhaṇḍ).
ex: اُس کے کوئی اَچّھے گُن ہَیں ہی نَہِیں۔
us ke koī acche gun ha͠i hī nahī̃.
He has no good qualities at all.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit गुण (guṇá).

def: s.m. (f. gunī) Fold, times (suffixed to numerals to form multiplicatives; e.g. sau-gunā, 'a hundred-fold'); — multiplication; the rule of multiplication
more: v. n. 1. To put in practice; bring into use. Paṛhe to haiṅ, par gune nahīṅ . Prov. He learns but does not apply what he learns. 2. ( Arith .) To multiply.
( In compos .) Fold; times, as chau gunā four times, do-gunā two-fold.
see گن guṇ, in composition denoting) Fold, turn, time; as, سوگنا sau-gunā, A hundred fold, centuple.
subs: s.m. Multiplication; — the product of multiplication
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

def: adj. See s.v. gūn. s.m. Colour; — kind, species; form, mode, fashion (used in comp., e.g. gul-gūṅ, adj. 'Rose-coloured, red')
more: adj. Of different colours or kinds, various, variegated, motley.
subs: adj. Of different colours; of different sorts or kinds; variegated; various; diversified; motley.

ety: Pārsī vanāh (cf. Pehl. venaś); rt. Zend nāś with vi; S. वि+नश्
def: s.m. Fault, offence, crime, sin; guilt; vice, iniquity (often contrac. to gunah, esp. in comp.)
more: n. m. Z. vanās . Sin ( pāp ). Uzr-i-gunāh, bad-tar az gunāh . P. Prov. An excuse for a sin is worse than the sin. gunāh bakhshnā , v. a. To pardon one's sin. gunāh-i-be-lazzat , n. m. 1. Sinning without pleasure. 2. A lie. gunāh sābit k. , v. a. To convict one of sin. gunāh se bachnā , v. n. To avoid sin. gunāh-i-sagīrah . A small sin. gunāh-i-azīm, yā kabīrah , A grievous sin. gunāh k. , v. a. To sin. gunāh-gār , n. m. Z. vanaskār . A sinner ( pāpī ). gunāh-gār , adj. Sinful. Pheroṅ kī gunāhgār hai . Prov. Saying. (Said of a child-widow). The marriage tie her only sin. (A Hin. widow is not permitted to marry again). gunāh-gār ṭhairānā , v. a. To condemn; sentence. gunāh-gārī , n. f. 1. Sinfulness; criminality; guiltiness. 2. Forfeit; mulct ( tāvāṅ ). 3. Loss; deficit. 4. Revenue derived from judicial fines. gunāh-gārī denā , v. a. To pay a fine, etc. be-gunāh , adj. Innocent; guiltless.…
s. m. A fault, crime, sin. گناه کرنا gunāh karnā, To sin, offend.
subs: (-par), To impute crime (to); to accuse falsely
To commit a fault, or sin; to offend; to sin

(or gunah-gār; in Hindī gunah-kār, and gunah-kārī), s.m. A sinner; an offender, a culprit, a criminal; — gunāh-gār ṭhairanā (-ko), To make (one) out (to be) an offender, or a sinner, &c.; — to convict one of crime or offence, &c.; to convict; to condemn; to sentence

(or gunah-gārī; — in Hindī gunah-kārī), s.f. A state of sin; a habit of vice; — sinfulness; criminality; guiltiness; — a fine for an offence; mulct, penalty; — revenue derived from judicial fines; — loss, deficit
To pay a fine or penalty
adj. Without offence, innocent, guiltless; sinless.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گناه (gunāh). Doublet of جُناح (junāh).

def: s. m. contract. of گناه gunāh, q. v.
s.m. contrac. of gunāh, q.v.: — gunah-gār: for this and other compounds see s.v. gunāh.

s. Criminal, culprit, guilty, sinner.

s. f. 1. Criminality, guiltiness. 2. Penalty, fine.
s. f. Mulct.
s.m. (in Arith.) The multiplier
s.m. See s.v. guṇ. s.m. Colour; — kind, species; form, mode, fashion (used in comp., e.g. gul-gūṅ, adj. 'Rose-coloured, red')
subs: adj. Of different colours; of different sorts or kinds; variegated; various; diversified; motley.

r: gunākar, guṇā-kār, guṇā, gunāh-gār, gunah-gār, gunāh
def: s.m. corr. 1˚ of guṇā-kār, q.v.s.v. guṇā; — 2˚ of gunāh-gār, or gunah-gār, q.v.s.v. gunāh.
s.m. Multiplication, the operation of multiplication; — counting, enumeration
s.m. The condition of a rope or string.
s.m. The condition of a rope or string.

s.m. A fop.

wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑁄𑀁𑀟 (goṃḍa) / गोंड (goṃḍa) / ಗೋಂಡ (goṃḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit गोण्ड (goṇḍa, “Gond”).

def: s.m. corr. of guṇḍā, q.v. s.m. A fop.
more: adj. Dissolute; rakish. gunḍā , n. m. gunḍī , n. f. 1. A bad, loose, or dissolute character. 1. Shahar kā gunḍā hai . Prov. He is a city rake. 2. Jo baiyar nikse dhore kar, gunḍoṅ ḍhāl use tāke . Rus. Wom. song. On every woman who goes by he fixes full his rakish eye. 3. Mo se mat bole, gunḍā, gārī toë dehūṅ, Bāt māī bahan ko sikhāo ras kī! Tū ne bin pūchhe aṅgiyā merī maskī . Wom. song. Dont speak to me, you rake! or I'll give you abuse, This prurient language to your mother, sister use! Without my leave to crush my boddice you must choose! 4. Na gunḍā gāoṅ ko bade, na gāoṅ gunḍe ko . Prov. The profligate has no regard for his village, nor the village for him. 2. A rascal; a rogue.

def: adj. Coarse, thick, large, gross.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Dumb, mute, speechless
more: adj. 1. Dumb. 2. Mute; speechless. 1. Gūṅge kā guṛ khaṭṭā na mīṭhā . Prov. A dumb man's sugar is neither sour nor sweet. (He cant tell). 2. Gūṅge ne supnā dekhā, man hī man pachhtāë . Prov. The dumb man has a dream, his mind with keen regrets doth teem. (He cant impart it). gūṅgī pahelī , n. f. A dumb riddle or charade in which signs are used for words. ☞ This used to be a favourite pastime in the seraglio of the king of Dehli. Thus, the finger on the forehead represents karam Fate, and the finger on the cheek points to kallā cheek—the two words together making karam-kallā a cabbage. Similarly, a rap with the knuckle stands for khaṭ (the sound), and one hand rubbed against the other for mal rub, the two together making khaṭ-mal a bug.
subs: s.f. A riddle asked in dumb-show (e.g. a finger placed on the forehead denotes karam, 'fate'; and the finger on the cheek signifies kallā, 'cheek': the two words together make karam-kallā, 'cabbage').

def: part. adj. (f. -ā), & adv. Protected, guarded, preserved; — hidden, concealed, kept secret; secret; withdrawn from sight, invisible; (in Gram.) not expressed, understood; — secretly, privately; — s.m. An appellation (of frequent use) forming the last member of compound proper names of men of the Vaiśya or third caste
more: adj. 1. Hidden ( poshīdah ). 2. Invisible. 3. ( Gram .) Understood (an ellipsis). gupt , adv. Secretly; privately. gupt-āmdanī , n. f. Income from a hidden source, as bribes, etc. gupt-dān , n. m. A gift made in secret. As when a person leaves property with a Brahman and never reclaims it, or presents him with a sealed bag, or drops a present in a sacred pool of water at Kurakshetra, in which the pilgrim has bathed at a solar eclipse, leaving the officiating Brahmans to search for it. gupt k. , v. a. To hide; conceal. gupt-mār , n. f. 1. Hurting so as to leave no marks. 2. Taunts; gibes; sneers. gupt māl , n. m. Hidden wealth or treasure. gupt h., gupt rahnā , v. n. To be invisible.
subs: s.f. Income from a hidden or secret source (as bribes, &c.)

s.m. A secret gift or present, a hidden donation; a valuable thing given in a concealed manner to Brāhmans or to a deity (as when a person leaves property with a Brāhman and never reclaims it; or presents him with a sealed bag; or drops a present in a sacred pool in which he has bathed at a solar eclipse, leaving the officiating Brāhmans to search for it)
v.n. To remain hidden, lie concealed; to be or continue invisible
s.f. A beating or hurt that leaves no marks; — taunt, gibe, sneer
s.m. A disguise, or dress, &c., used for concealment; — a false or deceitful person; a hypocrite, dissembler; an actor, a mimic (syn. ćhalī, kaptī; bahu-rūpī)
v.n. To be hidden or concealed; to be invisible.
wety: From Sanskrit गुप्त (gupta).

def: s.f. Protecting, protection; concealing, concealment; a means of protection.
more: n. f. A sword-stick. guptī chalānā , v. a. To stab secretly.
s. f. (dakh.) A foil to fence with, a small sword, a sword stick. گپتي چلانا guptī chalānā, To fence with swords ( Harris ).

def: s.f. A hidden sword; a sword-stick.
s.m. = گرو guru, q.v.
more: n. m. S. गुरु A rule of Arithmetic; a short method; a formula.

n. m. S. गुड Molasses; treacle; the produce of the first inspissation of the juice of the cane. 1. Chorī kā guṛ mīṭhā . Prov. Stolen treacle is sweet. (Stolen kisses are the sweetest). 2. Jahāṅ guṛ hogā vahāṅ makkhiyāṅ bhinkeṅgī . Prov. Where treacle is the flies will buzz. 3. Guṛ khāeṅ, gulguloṅ se parhez! Prov. Sugar he eats, sweet cakes he shuns! (Inconsistency). 4. Guṛ dīye mare to zahar kyoṅ dīje? Prov. If sugar will kill why give poison? 5. Guṛ na de to guṛ kī sī bāt to kahe . Prov. If sweets you cannot give sweet your discourse might be. 6. Thailī meṅ rupaiyā yā muṅh meṅ guṛ . Prov. Or money in your bag, or sweetness on your tongue. 7. Gāṅḍe se ganḍerī piyārī. guṛ se piyārā gāṅḍā, Mā bahan se jorā piyārī jis se hoë gujārā . Wom. Dearer than sugarcane the joint that's sown, than sugar sugarcane, Than mother, sister dear the wife who shares your joy and pain. 8. Achchhī bhaī! guṛ sa…
adj. or s. m. See گرو guru.
subs: (fem. gurvi, or gurviṇī), Heavy, weighty; great, large, extended, long (as a vowel), grave (as an accent); difficult, hard; high (in degree), vehement, violent, excessive, deep, much; important, serious, momentous; valuable, highly prized, dear, beloved; venerable, honourable, respectable; excellent; best; — one who is an object of reverence, a venerable or respectable person (as a father, mother, &c.); a spiritual parent or preceptor (from whom a youth receives the initiatory Mantra or prayer and who conducts the necessary ceremonies up to the period of investiture with the sacrificial thread); a spiritual guide, a religious teacher (one who explains the law and religion to his pupil, instructs him in the Śāstras, &c.); a pastor; a teacher (generally); a superior; — a name of Brihaspati or the planet Jupiter (who is considered as the guru or preceptor of the gods); — a wise man, a sage ( = gur-gyānī); — (ironic.) a consummate knave
vulg. gur-bhāʼī, s.m. Follower of the same spiritual preceptor or guide, fellow-sectarian, fellow-disciple; fellow-student
s.m. (f. -ī), The child (natural or adopted) of a religious preceptor

vulg. gur-jan, s.m. pl. Religious or venerable persons, eminent persons, great people, respectable people; — s.m. sing. A venerable person (as a father, a religious preceptor, &c.), an elder, a superior
vulg. gur-daććhanā, s.f. A fee given to a spiritual preceptor; rent-free land given to a religious teacher
s.m. The pronouncing a blessing on a religious guide
s.m. A respectful epithet of any guru
s.m. The eighth Nakshatra or lunar constellation Pushya (of which Brihaspati is the ruling deity)
s.m. A serious or momentous affair; — the business or office of a spiritual teacher
s.m. 'A guru with bells' (hung round his neck), a thorough knave
vulg. guru-laćh, or gur-laćh, s.m. = next
adj. (f. -ā), Heavy, hard of digestion; — s.m. Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only
s.f. The wife of a spiritual teacher
(or gur-) mukh honā, v.n. To receive the initiatory Mantra from a guru; to become a disciple or pupil
s.f. Name of the character in which the Panjābī language is written
vulg. gur-bār, s.m. 'The day of Jupiter,' Thursday.
